<!-- generated by sofar — do not hand-edit; truth lives in events.jsonl -->

# Decisions: felt-cost

- 2026-07-12T12:13:31.569Z — chose **Cycle scope = the memo's three vetted plays (cold-resume advisory, rent-meter with cache-read health bands, batch-complete nudge behind SPEC) plus a byte-stability regression test as the residue of the report's cache-anchoring play 1a** over Building cache-anchoring as new work, or adopting report Tier-2 (model-tiered/batch bookkeeping, delta injection) because The 1a property already exists (renderStatus embeds no volatile bytes) — pin it against silent regression rather than build what's already true. Report Stage-1 hardening confirmed already shipped (independent confirmation the engine was built right, not wasted research). User-approved Jul 12.
- 2026-07-12T12:13:40.584Z — chose **Any future cache-cost play must cite token-optimization's rejected approach "leading with prompt caching (doesn't reduce occupancy)" as an informed re-test — legitimate because the scope differs (dollar cost vs token occupancy)** over Treating the recorded rejection as blocking, or ignoring it as out-of-scope because The M4 convention is the M4 convention: informed re-tests that cite the recorded decision are legitimate; silent re-proposal is not. The record governing its own evolution is the product working on itself. User ruling Jul 12.
- 2026-07-12T12:13:48.974Z — chose **"sofar makes zero model API calls" promoted to a named architectural invariant (docs/SPEC.md + CLAUDE.md guard-rails); report Tier-2 (Haiku/Batch bookkeeping, 2a/2b) deferred entirely** over Adopting model-tiered or Batch-API bookkeeping, or leaving the property implicit as an accident of implementation because No API keys, no inference costs, no user content sent anywhere; write-backs are already minimal by construction because they are the agent's own tool-call args (report 2c true by construction). Trading that for the measured ~$3.87/2-day upside would be a terrible exchange. User ruling Jul 12.
- 2026-07-12T12:25:52.765Z — chose **sofar statusline (3.1 SPEC-lite): a read-side CLI subcommand for Claude Code's statusLine slot — reads statusline JSON from stdin, prints ONE plain line "slug done/total · $cost · cache N%[⚠|✓] · ctx N%", omits any segment it cannot compute, appends nothing to the record, always exits 0. Health marker judged only after 10k tokens: &lt;30% warm ⚠ (prefix nondeterminism), ≥50% ✓. Root resolution: --root/cwd, falling back to the JSON's workspace.current_dir then cwd** over Auto-installing the statusLine in sofar init (clobbers user config — README documents the one-liner instead), ui-kernel/ANSI styling (statusline is a contract-byte surface, keep plain), or serving cost via the localhost state server (statusline stdin already carries cost + cache tokens at zero API cost) because The rent-meter must satisfy the zero-model-API invariant: pure stdin→stdout derivation, no keys, no calls. A statusline must never break or mutate — hooks' best-effort philosophy (BD22) applies verbatim. Bands from the Jul-12 research: healthy stable-prefix workloads run 50–80% cache-read; below 30% signals prefix non-determinism — itself a sofar self-diagnostic.
- 2026-07-12T12:31:55.769Z — chose **Batch-complete nudge (4.1 SPEC): a fifth shim, user-prompt-submit.sh → `sofar event user-prompt` (UserPromptSubmit). When the session is registered AND initiative drift since the last write-back ≥5 mechanical events, emit ONE line of additionalContext nudging sofar_end_session while context is warm; else silence. Stateless and re-fires each prompt until write-back (staleness-line precedent). Mid-session repeat write-backs are blessed: fold is already last-wins (ended/summary/next_action overwritten, freshness resets, Stop passes once any session_ended exists)** over Nudging via the Stop hook only (too late — the forced block costs an extra cold turn, $0.65/block measured), a stateful once-per-batch memory (violates read-side derivation), or nudging unregistered sessions (noise in unbound/foreign repos) because UserPromptSubmit stdout at exit 0 becomes additionalContext that lands AFTER the cached prefix (cache-safe, per the Jul-12 research); write-backs that happen in-flow while context is warm make the Stop gate a fallback instead of a tax. Best-effort per BD22: any failure is silence, never a blocked prompt.
- 2026-07-12T12:56:26.725Z — chose **Restore the default-statusline information INSIDE sofar statusline (D4 amendment): prepend model display name and dir:branch segments — model.display_name from stdin JSON; dir = basename of workspace.current_dir (cwd fallback); branch read from .git/HEAD by bounded upward walk (gitdir-file/worktree aware), no subprocess. Order: model · dir:branch · record · $ · cache · ctx; same independence + omission rules** over A wrapper script composing sofar's output with hand-rolled default segments (pushes maintenance onto every user), or documenting the loss as intended (the rent-meter should never cost the user the line they had) because Claude Code renders exactly one statusLine command — configuring sofar's replaced the default line and its model/dir/branch info (user report, Jul 12). All three segments derive from the same stdin JSON plus one local file read: zero API cost, zero subprocess, best-effort like every other segment.
- 2026-07-12T13:01:04.092Z — chose **Styled statusline (D7): the statusline is a HUMAN surface whose consumer (Claude Code's status bar) renders ANSI + emoji even though stdout is piped — so the command wiring FORCES styled caps (bold model, 📁 dir 🌿 branch, accent slug, band-colored cache, threshold-colored ctx ≥70% warn / ≥90% error, dim separators) instead of TTY detection; --no-color or NO_COLOR env falls back to the plain line, byte-identical to 0.8.0. Icons gate on the unicode cap, colors on the color cap; runStatusline's library default stays plain** over TTY-detected caps (piped output would always strip styles — the one wrong answer for a status bar), emoji-free ANSI only (user asked for icons), or a separate styled variant command because User feedback on 0.8.0: the line renders as bare basics. The plain-surface guard protects AGENT surfaces; sofar status/list/doctor already style human surfaces via the ui kernel — the statusline belongs with them, and the kernel costs zero new dependencies (cli-ui D1/D2).
- 2026-07-12T13:04:57.102Z — chose **Statusline icons are text GLYPHS from the house vocabulary, not emoji (D7 amendment, user feedback): ▸ dir, ⎇ branch, ↺ cache, and the kernel's honest progress pie (○◔◑◕●) as the context gauge; ✓/⚠ markers unchanged** over Emoji icons (📁 🌿 ♻ 🧠 — render as color pictographs, off-register with the CLI's glyph language), or nerd-font glyphs (require patched fonts) because The sofar CLI already speaks a cp437-fallback-safe glyph vocabulary (cli-ui 1.3) — the statusline should be the same product, same register; the pie is literally built for fill-gauges and ctx% is a fill.
- 2026-07-12T13:10:27.719Z — chose **Task-progress pie on the statusline record segment (D9, user feedback): `<pie> <slug> <done>/<total>` with the next.ts coloring convention — success when complete, warn in progress, dim untouched; glyph mode only (plain line stays byte-compatible). Ships in the still-unpublished 0.8.1** over Numbers-only record segment (the 5.1 shape), or reserving the pie for ctx alone to avoid two pies per line because pieFor exists precisely to quantize done/total (cli-ui 4.2) and sofar next already leads entries with it — the statusline should read as the same product; position + adjacent text (slug d/t vs %) disambiguate the two gauges.
- 2026-07-12T13:13:40.706Z — chose **Cache segment keeps its text label in every mode — `cache N% [✓|⚠]` (D10, user feedback); the ↺ glyph is dropped** over The ↺ rewarm glyph from D8 because The word carries the meaning; ↺ reads as refresh/undo, not cache-hit share. Band marks ✓/⚠ stay — they are state, not decoration.
- 2026-07-12T13:33:50.474Z — chose **Statusline ctx gauge keeps its text label in every mode — `ctx N%` (D10 word-over-glyph extended to the context segment, user feedback); healthy band (<70%) tones success-green instead of dim; model name toned by family inside the D1 semantic palette: Fable bold accent, Opus accent, Sonnet info, Haiku success, unknown families keep the D6 bold** over The D8 progress-pie ctx gauge with a dim healthy band, and the uniform bold model segment of D6/D7 because Same ruling as D10: the word carries the meaning — the pie next to a bare percent read as decoration, not a gauge. Color-coding the two most-glanced segments (which model, how full) makes them legible without reading; every tone stays ANSI-16 semantic so the terminal theme supplies the palette. Plain (--no-color) line unchanged, still byte-identical to 0.8.0.
